title: The Architecture Milestone
concept: PHASE D9 COMPLETE. We have mastered parameter scaling, sparse routing, attention optimization, and multimodal projection. Boss explain ends with: 'Phase D9: LLM Architectures & Variants is complete. Next up: Evaluating LLMs.'

exercises.prompt: What major components have we explored in modern LLM architectures using been combined to form-level AI systems have we explored to build advanced, modern AI architectures?

options.text: Only simple text blocks without any structure
Mixtures of experts, optimized attention, and multimodal connectors
Traditional computer keyboards and mouse inputs

exercises.explain: Modern LLMs use specialized components like MoE for sparse routing, optimized attention for speed, and multimodal layers for processing images.

cards.title: The Master Blueprint
cards.body: We have traveled through the blueprints of advanced AI! From scaling up parameters to routing tokens through specialized experts, and teaching models to see images. Let's celebrate wrapping up this major architectural phase!

cards.title: Defining the Architecture Milestone
cards.body: The Architecture Milestone represents the integration of advanced LLM structural designs. By combining parameter scaling, Mixture of Experts (sparse routing), optimized attention, and multimodal connectors, we can build custom AI models tailored for diverse, real-world tasks.

cards.title: The Modular Skyscraper
cards.body: Imagine building a custom skyscraper. Instead of using the exact same blueprint for a library, an apartment, and a warehouse, you swap out components—adding cargo elevators for heavy goods, or extra windows for views. Similarly, AI engineers mix and match components like sparse routing or multimodal projectors to build the perfect model for a specific job.

cards.title: Architecture in Action
cards.body: A modern AI assistant uses a Mixture of Experts (MoE) routing system to quickly write code, switches to a multimodal projector to analyze a uploaded photo, and relies on flash attention to read a massive PDF file without lagging. All these systems work together in one unified architecture.

cards.title: Myth: Bigger is Always Better
cards.body: People often think that to make an AI smarter, you just have to make it bigger. In reality, smart architectural choices—like routing active signals only to specific expert networks—make models faster, cheaper, and much more efficient without just adding raw size.

cards.title: Uniform vs. Modular
cards.body: Compare a traditional uniform model with a modern modular architecture.

left.text: Every input passes through every single parameter, requiring massive computing power.

right.text: Inputs are routed only to specialized parts (like MoE or visual layers) when needed.

exercises.prompt: What is the main role of a multimodal projector in an LLM architecture?

options.text: It translates the entire model's code into Spanish.
It translates visual tokens so the text-based LLM can understand them.
It deletes all text to make the model run faster.
It physically projects the AI's screen onto a wall.

exercises.explain: Multimodal projectors act as translation bridges that map image or audio tokens into the same vector space that the text model understands.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Attention optimization techniques help models process long texts faster while using less memory.
exercises.explain: Flash attention and other attention optimizations are specifically designed to reduce memory usage and speed up processing for long documents.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how modern models save computation:
exercises.sentence: Instead of activating the whole network, sparse routing sends tokens to specialized sub-networks called experts.
exercises.explain: Mixture of Experts (MoE) architectures route tokens to specialized subnetworks (experts) to save computing power.

exercises.prompt: Which of these statements is describing an AI model architecture?

options.text: I am going to hire three human specialists to write this software application.
We deployed a sparse routing layer that directs input tokens to 2 out of 8 feed-forward expert networks.
I am going to print this blueprint on a large poster board for the client meeting.

exercises.explain: The second option represents an AI because it mentions sparse routing and token activation, which are key concepts in Mixture of Experts (MoE) architectures.

exercises.prompt: We have reached the end of our architectural journey! How do modern engineers build specialized, powerful LLMs for different tasks?

options.text: They use a single, unchanging blueprint for every task.
They combine custom modules like sparse routing, attention styles, and multimodal layers.
They only write code in plain English without using neural networks.
They avoid using parameters entirely to save computer memory.

exercises.explain: With these architectural variations mastered, Phase D9: LLM Architectures & Variants is complete. Next up: Evaluating LLMs. We will soon test how well these different designs actually perform on real-world tasks!
